title: Engineering topological flat bands in $Γ$-valley moiré systems with Ising-type SOC: twisted 1T-ZrS$_2$ and 1T-SnSe$_2$
authors: Hanqi Pi, Yves H. Kwan, Haoyu Hu, Yi Jiang, Dumitru Călugăru, Jie Shan, Kin Fai Mak, Miguel M. Ugeda, Dmitri K. Efetov, Maia G. Vergniory, B. Andrei Bernevig
abstract: Twisted moiré superlattices hosting topological flat bands provide a platform to explore the interplay between topology and correlations. Here we investigate topological band structures in $Γ$-valley moiré systems based on 1T-ZrS$_2$ and 1T-SnSe$_2$. Using large-scale ab initio calculations and continuum modelling, we demonstrate that both materials exhibit an approximate spin-$U(1)$ symmetry and host isolated topological moiré valence bands, including quantum spin Hall and high spin Chern states. By constructing a hierarchy of $Γ$-valley moiré continuum models, we show that isolated moiré bands carry a trivial $C_3$ symmetry indicator when the low-energy physics is described by a single effective orbital and a single layer-hybridized branch, either bonding or antibonding. Topological bands therefore arise from inter-branch and/or inter-orbital coupling. Moreover, we determine interaction-driven phase diagrams using Hartree--Fock and exact diagonalization, finding various phases tunable by twist angle, interaction strength, and displacement field. We identify specific conditions under which fractional Chern insulators are favored. Together with previous work showing that the moiré conduction bands of 1T-ZrS$_2$ and 1T-SnSe$_2$ realize $M$-valley twisting and host quasi-one-dimensional physics, our results establish these systems as ideal platforms for strongly correlated moiré physics and provide a systematic framework for understanding topological band structures in $Γ$-valley moiré materials.
summary: This paper investigates twisted 1T-ZrS2 and 1T-SnSe2 moiré systems as platforms for topological physics. It shows that inter-orbital and inter-branch coupling generates isolated topological bands, which can host various interaction-driven phases such as Chern insulators and fractional Chern insulators.
main_problem: The study aims to identify new moiré platforms hosting isolated topological flat bands and to understand the microscopic origin of topology and interaction-driven phases in Gamma-valley moiré systems.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that twisted 1T-ZrS2 and 1T-SnSe2 host isolated topological moiré valence bands, including quantum spin Hall and high spin Chern states, and predict various interaction-driven phases like fractional Chern insulators.
method: The research utilizes large-scale ab initio DFT calculations, symmetry-constrained continuum modeling, and many-body methods including Hartree-Fock and Exact Diagonalization.
model_or_system: The system consists of twisted bilayer 1T-ZrS2 (AA and AB stacking) and 1T-SnSe2 (AB stacking) moiré superlattices, characterized by Gamma-valley physics and Ising-type spin-orbit coupling.
key_observables: Spin Chern number (Cs), spin-projected Wilson loops, C3 symmetry indicators, spin-polarized Chern insulators, and fractional Chern insulators (FCIs).
important_parameters: Twist angle, interaction strength (10/epsilon_r), displacement field (D), and filling factor (nu).
main_assumptions: The use of a continuum model approximation and the leading-harmonic approximation for the moiré potential.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows crystal structures and monolayer band structures with orbital projections; Figure 2 compares DFT and continuum model moiré band structures.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the materials and electronic structure, develops a hierarchy of continuum models based on symmetry, analyzes single-particle topological properties, and concludes with many-body phase diagrams using Hartree-Fock and Exact Diagonalization.
why_interesting: The paper explores the emergence of topological order and fractionalized states in a system with reduced spin symmetry (spin-U(1)), which is highly relevant to the study of many-body dynamics and topological phases in correlated systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Crystal structure and orbital-resolved band structures of monolayer 1T-SnSe2 and 1T-ZrS2. (a) Top and side views of the 1T-TMD crystal structure. (b, c) Band structures of monolayer (b) ZrS2 and (c) SnSe2 calculated without (left subpanels) and with (right subpanels) spin-orbit coupling (SOC). The red and blue dots represent the projection weights of the chalcogen in-plane (px, py) and out-of-plane pz orbitals, respectively. (d) Schematic illustrating the influence of the IRREP dimension on the Γ states. Top: The state contributed by pz orbital transforms as a 1D IRREP (left) without SOC, which preserves an approximate spin-SU(2) symmetry near Γ and evolves into a single degenerate...

FIG. 2. Electronic and topological properties of twisted bilayer ZrS2 and SnSe2. (a–c) Band structures for (a) AA-stacked ZrS2 at 3.89◦, (b) AB-stacked ZrS2 at 3.89◦, and (c) AB-stacked SnSe2 at 4.41◦. Black dashed lines and blue solid lines represent the results from DFT calculations and the first harmonic continuum model with parameters in Tables S9 to S11, respectively. The color intensity indicates the wavefunction overlap between the DFT and continuum model states. The C3z eigenvalue of ΓM and KM are listed near the momentum. (d–f) The spin-projected Wilson loop for the topmost isolated bands in (a–c). (g–i) Local density of states (LDOS) distributions of the topmost isolated bands for...

FIG. 3. Triple-Q description of isolated moiré bands in spin-U(1) -symmetric Γ-valley systems. Green solid and purple dashed curves denote spin-up and spin-down bands, respectively, and the C3z eigenvalues annotated next to top bands are color- matched to their spin sector. mJ is the angular momentum of the spinful orbital, and Πz ∈{0, ±1} is the Q-lattice angular momentum. (a) Moiré Brillouin zone with the three nearest Q-sites Q(j) around KM and Q′(j) = −Q(j) around K′ M. (b) Schematic band structure of layer group p3 in the single-orbital-single-branch limit, and the constraint Eq. (3) pins the topmost isolated Kramers pair to Cs = 0 mod 3. (c) Schematic band structure of layer group...

FIG. 4. Hartree–Fock (HF) phase diagrams at integer fillings and exact-diagonalization (ED) results at fractional fillings. Panels (a–f) show HF phase diagrams. White regions denote gapless phases. Red and grey regions denote gapped phases with Chern number C = 1 and C = 0, respectively. Light shaded regions indicate phases with inter-spin coherence (ISC). In regions with finite ISC marked by dashed outlines, “Z2 TI” denotes a topological insulator with broken spin-U(1) symmetry, in contrast to the QSH1 phase which preserves spin-U(1) . Panels (a, d) correspond to AA-stacked ZrS2, (b, e) to AB-stacked ZrS2, and (c, f) to AB-stacked SnSe2. The top row (a–c) shows ν = −1, and the bottom row...
